pattern: A beautiful partition never benefits from spanning more than two characters at a time, so the whole answer collapses to independently fixing up each consecutive pair of positions.
hints: Any valid partition only needs even-length, single-character blocks, and it turns out you never need a block longer than two characters -- larger uniform blocks can always be re-split into pairs without costing anything extra.
So look at the string two characters at a time: positions (0,1), (2,3), (4,5), and so on.
Each pair already forms a valid two-character block on its own if both characters match; otherwise exactly one change fixes that pair -- sum those mismatches across all pairs for the answer.

edge_inputs.description: already beautiful with alternating pairs of blocks
a long already-beautiful string made of large uniform runs
